hook.text: The Agile Manifesto fits on a single page — just four value statements.

buildup.text: Individuals over processes. Working software over documentation. Collaboration over contracts.

discovery.text: The fourth value: responding to change over following a plan. Heresy to traditionalists.

twist.text: The manifesto didn't say the right side was worthless — just that the left side mattered more.

climax.text: Four sentences captured what decades of methodology debates had missed: start with people.

punchline.text: The shortest manifesto changed the most minds.

quiz.question: What does the Agile Manifesto value over following a plan?
